title: Fullstack Web App Harness
summary: Pipeline-oriented fullstack delivery harness for requirements, architecture, frontend, backend, QA, and deployment preparation.

orchestrator.name: fullstack-webapp
orchestrator.description: Use when the user asks for a fullstack web app harness, a role-based website delivery workflow, or a project structure for frontend, backend, QA, and deployment work.

activation_examples: 이 저장소에 맞는 웹서비스 개발 팀 설계해줘
프론트엔드, 백엔드, QA, 배포까지 나눠서 일하는 구조 만들어줘

mode_matrix.notes: Default mode for greenfield or major feature delivery.
Skip frontend and deployment when the user only needs API or data-layer work.
Assume an existing API contract or mock service.
Use a narrow infra mode when the product code already exists.

phases.name: Phase 1: Intake and Architecture
steps: Extract product goal, key flows, target users, stack constraints, and whether the repository is greenfield or incremental.
Inspect the current repository and write the normalized brief to `_workspace/00_input.md`.
Have the architect define system shape, API boundaries, database model, and delivery assumptions in `_workspace/01_architecture.md`, `_workspace/02_api_spec.md`, and `_workspace/03_db_schema.md`.

phases.name: Phase 2: Parallel Delivery
steps: Once architecture is stable, run frontend, backend, and deployment preparation in parallel where possible.
Have each implementation role write concrete artifacts and flag contract mismatches quickly.
Keep QA informed of architecture and interface decisions so review criteria are ready before integration.

phases.name: Phase 3: QA and Finalization
steps: Have QA validate functionality, interface coherence, and regression risk, then write `_workspace/04_test_plan.md` and `_workspace/06_review_report.md`.
Have DevOps finalize deployment notes in `_workspace/05_deploy_guide.md`.
Return final deliverables with clear residual risks, follow-up work, and readiness status.

supporting_skills.name: component-patterns
supporting_skills.description: Frontend component and state-management patterns for the frontend developer.

supporting_skills.name: api-security-checklist
supporting_skills.description: Backend API hardening checklist for auth, validation, and unsafe defaults.

agents.name: architect
agents.description: Define system shape, data boundaries, API contracts, and execution order so implementation roles can proceed with minimal ambiguity.

agents.name: frontend-dev
agents.description: Implement pages, UI components, client-side state, and API integration according to the agreed architecture.
focus_areas: API integration and resilient client state

agents.name: backend-dev
agents.description: Implement API routes, data access, authentication, authorization, and business rules according to the agreed contracts.

agents.name: qa-engineer
agents.description: Validate interface coherence, test strategy, regression risk, and readiness for release.
focus_areas: Interface mismatches between frontend and backend
Critical user-flow coverage and regression risk
Readiness gates for release or handoff

agents.name: devops-engineer
agents.description: Prepare deployment, CI or release automation, environment assumptions, and operational handoff guidance.

reference_harnesses.reason: Closest existing Codex harness for multi-role website delivery with architecture-first sequencing.
